evals.name: audit-staged-no-disclosure-no-original
evals.prompt: I'm about to put this virtually-staged photo (fixtures/staged.png) on my California MLS listing. It has no caption and I haven't attached the original. Am I compliant?
evals.expected_output: A compliance-report.md that marks the staged photo FIX, cites AB-723 for (a) the missing conspicuous disclosure and (b) the missing/unpaired unaltered original, and lists both concrete fixes; OVERALL = FIX; nothing published.

assertions.text: The staged photo is marked FIX (not PASS)
The report cites AB-723 for a missing conspicuous disclosure
The report flags the missing/unpaired unaltered original
The report states it never publishes and is not legal advice

evals.name: audit-compliant-pass
evals.prompt: Check this listing photo set for California AB-723 compliance. The altered file is fixtures/staged-disclosed.jpg (it already has a 'Virtually Staged' caption), its unaltered original is fixtures/empty.png, and I'll publish them adjacent in this order: empty.png, staged-disclosed.jpg.
evals.expected_output: A compliance-report.md that marks the altered photo PASS — disclosure present (vision), original present + adjacent + public, and the edit is 'better not different' (staging an empty room).

assertions.text: The altered photo is marked PASS
The disclosure-present check passes via vision (caption is visible)
The better-not-different judge returns BETTER for staging an empty room

evals.name: audit-misrepresentation-different
evals.prompt: Audit this edit for compliance. fixtures/empty.png is the original; fixtures/staged.png is the edited version I want to post (treat any change that hides a defect or alters a permanent feature as a problem).
evals.expected_output: The better-not-different judge compares the two; if the edit only stages/decorates it returns BETTER, but the report explains the DIFFERENT criteria (hidden defects, changed dimensions, added/removed permanent features) and would FIX a misrepresentation.

assertions.text: The report applies the better-not-different judge over the original/edited pair
The report explains what would make an edit DIFFERENT/misrepresentation (defect-hiding, dimensions, permanent features)
Defaults toward FIX/REVIEW rather than silently passing when uncertain
